failure_modes.text: A team can mistake reciprocity pressure for genuine agreement and push people toward yes before they have judged the merits.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when reciprocity pressure creates obligation without informed evaluation
failure_modes.mitigation: Separate social pressure from judgment: after any reciprocity-driven exchange, ask whether the person would still agree if the prior favor had never occurred.

failure_modes.text: Goodwill can be destroyed quickly if early promises or professionalism signals are not delivered faultlessly.
failure_modes.source_quote: you must do it faultlessly and without fail-on time, no excuses
failure_modes.mitigation: Deliver on promises with visible reliability: treat early commitments as trust-building investments whose failure cost is disproportionately high.

failure_modes.text: Relationship-building can slide into manipulation if influence work is not undertaken planfully and transparently.
failure_modes.source_quote: The persuasive power of reciprocity can be leveraged to frame a situation deceptively.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit influence tactics for transparency: before deploying reciprocity as a persuasion strategy, check whether the approach would survive public disclosure without damaging trust.

premortem_questions.text: If the other side accepts now, is the acceptance coming from thoughtful analysis or from the pressure to reciprocate?
premortem_questions.source_quote: is their acceptance based on thoughtful, deliberate analysis of the outcomes (System 2), or merely on the psychological pressure to reciprocate

premortem_questions.text: Have we actually delivered every promise on time, or are we leaning on past goodwill to excuse current defects?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Have we delivered every single thing we promised to do for the client so far, on time and without fail

premortem_questions.text: Are we trying to create a reciprocal relationship or just a compliant transaction?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Are we confusing the goal of generating a sale with the deeper goal of creating a reciprocal relationship

heuristics.text: Lead with real value before asking for commitment so the exchange is anchored in usefulness rather than in obligation theater.
heuristics.source_quote: The more value you give, the more value you get

heuristics.text: Require skin in the game when seeking expert help so reciprocal effort is tied to serious problem ownership.
heuristics.source_quote: you should demonstrate that you have already invested time, energy, and money into solving the problem

heuristics.text: Keep the relationship authentic and explicit enough that reciprocity supports trust instead of bypassing judgment.

curation_notes.summary: The source is strongest on value-first trust building, faultless follow-through, and the ethics line between reciprocity and manipulation.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor influence sprawl beyond the raw source's clearer relationship, professionalism, and anti-manipulation material.
open_questions: The first premortem question compresses the source's System 1 versus System 2 warning into a reusable intervention check.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on liking, unity, and consistency pressure in early relationship formation.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is strongest on critical-thinking and deliberative evaluation.
structured_tensions: The source contains strong pressure against critical-thinking but not a current-vocabulary explicit structured tension block.
